quotes.quote_text: he,--“did it _ soon _ make you think better of me ? Did you , on reading it , give any credit to its contents ? ”

context.quote_text: “ Oh , do not repeat what I then said . These recollections will not do at all . I assure you that I have long been most heartily ashamed of it . ”

“ that what I wrote must give you pain , but it was necessary . I hope you have destroyed the letter . There was one part , especially the opening of it , which I should dread your having the power of reading again . I can remember some expressions which might justly make you hate me . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ The letter shall certainly be burnt , if you believe it essential to the preservation of my regard ; but , though we have both reason to think my opinions not entirely unalterable , they are not , I hope , quite so easily changed as that implies . ”

context.quote_text: “ that what I wrote must give you pain , but it was necessary . I hope you have destroyed the letter . There was one part , especially the opening of it , which I should dread your having the power of reading again . I can remember some expressions which might justly make you hate me . ”
“ I believed myself perfectly calm and cool ; but I am since convinced that it was written in a dreadful bitterness of spirit . ”
